subOutputs.agent: Prices and Cost of Living Agent
key_findings: Core CPI latest value is 332.793 on 2026-01-01
Rent index latest value is 441.285 on 2026-01-01
risks: Blunt subsidies can raise prices if supply is fixed, focus on targeted and time limited design
Housing actions take time, set expectations and pair with near term stabilization steps
actions_60_90_days: Target short term relief to rent burdened households using time limited support tied to income and local rent indexes
Increase near term housing supply throughput by pushing federal permitting support and technical assistance to cities that approve multi family starts
Enforce competition rules in concentrated consumer categories, prioritize price transparency and anti price gouging investigations where evidence exists
Reduce month to month cost pressures by expanding access to energy assistance and utility bill smoothing programs for vulnerable households

claims.claim: Latest Core CPI pulled from FRED
Latest rent index pulled from FRED

key_findings: Unemployment rate latest value is 4.3 on 2026-01-01
Payroll employment latest value is 158627 on 2026-01-01
risks: Overheating demand can worsen inflation, pair job support with supply side actions
Poorly targeted programs waste money, require metrics and regional triggers
actions_60_90_days: Accelerate hiring in critical public service roles using time boxed fast track hiring and retention bonuses where shortages are documented
Expand rapid reemployment support in high layoff sectors through short credential programs tied to local employer demand
Use procurement and small business lending levers to protect jobs in regions showing weakening labor indicators
Coordinate with the central bank on messaging, avoid policy whiplash that damages hiring confidence

key_findings: Regular gas price latest value is 3.015 on 2026-03-02
risks: Energy shocks can feed headline inflation quickly
actions_60_90_days: If verified supply disruption exists, coordinate short term stabilization steps with energy agencies and states
Increase price transparency and enforcement focus in regions showing abnormal spreads between wholesale and retail prices
Expand targeted energy assistance for households facing utility and fuel burden

claims.claim: Latest regular gas price pulled from FRED

key_findings: No live transport cost series pulled in this minimal build
risks: Port or trucking disruptions can raise prices and delay supply
actions_60_90_days: Clear logistics bottlenecks by expanding port hours and simplifying inspections where safe
Support trucker capacity with temporary operational fixes and targeted maintenance funding
Prioritize repairs on choke points that reduce delivery times for food and essentials

claims.claim: No transport evidence included in this run

finalOut.question: Gas prices are up and rent feels high. What should the president do in the next 60 to 90 days?
recommendations_60_90_days: Target short term relief to rent burdened households using time limited support tied to income and local rent indexes
Increase near term housing supply throughput by pushing federal permitting support and technical assistance to cities that approve multi family starts
Enforce competition rules in concentrated consumer categories, prioritize price transparency and anti price gouging investigations where evidence exists
Reduce month to month cost pressures by expanding access to energy assistance and utility bill smoothing programs for vulnerable households
Accelerate hiring in critical public service roles using time boxed fast track hiring and retention bonuses where shortages are documented
Expand rapid reemployment support in high layoff sectors through short credential programs tied to local employer demand
Use procurement and small business lending levers to protect jobs in regions showing weakening labor indicators
Coordinate with the central bank on messaging, avoid policy whiplash that damages hiring confidence
tradeoffs_and_risks: Blunt subsidies can raise prices if supply is fixed, focus on targeted and time limited design
Housing actions take time, set expectations and pair with near term stabilization steps
Overheating demand can worsen inflation, pair job support with supply side actions
Poorly targeted programs waste money, require metrics and regional triggers
Energy shocks can feed headline inflation quickly
Port or trucking disruptions can raise prices and delay supply
what_to_measure_next: Monthly CPI and core CPI trend
Weekly gasoline price benchmarks if available
Unemployment rate and labor force participation
Rent inflation indicators from private and public sources
